flares.note: There is a simultaneous, longer duration significant flare from Active region 14425 (N06E52) contributing to this event.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-26T01:48Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, and briefly by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a downlink data gap from 2026-04-25T18:38Z to 2026-04-26T02:38Z. The source of this CME is an M1.3 class flare from Active Region 14425 (N06E64) which peaked at 2026-04-26T00:23Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Opening field lines and post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 94, 171 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-26T08:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is a small and faint region of dimming seen located at approx. S40W30 begging at approx. 2026-04-26T07:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint, very slowly developing CME resembling a streamer blowout that is seen to the NNW in GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO coronagraphs. It is very faint in difference imagery of COR2 but more pronounced in COR2 white light imagery. Its start time is likely much earlier. No clear source has been found for this event, so is possibly far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this faint front in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for this CME to confirm this analysis.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2, with limited SOHO LASCO cadence due to comet observations. The source is best observed as wide breaking of field lines over the central west limb in SDO AIA 171/193 starting around 2026-04-27T01:43Z. STEREO A EUVI 195 returns from a data gap at 2026-04-27T02:25Z and misses the main eruption, but post eruptive brightening SW of former AR 14419 is observed, pointing to the likely source here around W105-W115.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between available SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery (due to ongoing comet campaign) and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2, fades quickly so not visible in other coronagraphs. The source is observed near AR 14426, as dimming and field line movement centered around N13W70 in SDO AIA 171/193 starting around 2026-04-27T13:21Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis with SOHO LASCO C2 using the observed source at N13W70 observing some northward deflection.

cmes.note: Extremely faint CME with a fan-like, unclear front seen to the S in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SW in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph, followed by follow-up faint fronts. Its source may be seen to the SSE in GOES SUVI and more faintly SDO AIA 304 after 2026-04-28T16Z as very slowly developing prominence on/behind the SSE limb, and as faint moving of field lines in SDO AIA 171 seen behind the SSE limb at the same time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate, somewhat speculative analysis based on the fit of the super faint and somewhat unclear front in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and a slightly more pronounced front in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely far-sided.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap from 2026-04-28T20:38Z to 2026-04-29T02:23Z. The source is an eruption centered near N17E26 starting around 2026-04-28T23:15Z which deflects to the north as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not made. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

cmes.note: Very faint CME first seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-04-29T21:12Z. This front is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and dissipated before leaving the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. It is not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source is possibly related to an eruption near the eastern limb best seen in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-04-29T20:19Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is likely an unnumbered active region near the SE limb, centered approximately at S18E80. A slow opening of field lines is observed from this region starting around 2026-04-30T04:51Z in SDO AIA 171, with some weak brightening in SDO AIA 193 following the field line movement. Triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs also corroborates this source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint, wide CME seen to the south-southwest in SOHO LASCO C2 and to the southeast more prominently in STEREO A COR2; the source is a likely instability below Active Regions 14427/14428 (~S30), with a slowly varying dimming signature starting after 10:00Z best viewed in SDO composite imagery, particularly the 304/211/171 Angstrom imager.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 before the CME's front disappears before reaching the field of view, the CME is still visible in COR2A until about 17:00Z, so the measurement is able to be taken out to about ten solar radii.

cmes.note: Second of two faint, wide CMEs seen to the south-southwest in SOHO LASCO C2 and to the southeast more prominently in STEREO A COR2; the source could be associated with a minor eruption in 14428 (~S24W11), with a slowly varying dimming signature starting after 18:00Z best viewed in SDO composite imagery, particularly the 304/211/171 Angstrom imager. Follows shortly behind CME: 2026-04-30T12:38Z which has a similar appearance. Triangulation between the SOHO and STEREO A spacecraft strongly suggests a front-sided source location, though the source is relatively uncertain.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary measurement of the leading edge using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 for triangulation, which suggests a front-sided source possibly associated with AR 4428. Appears to be more northward than the earlier CME occurring before it, though of very similar velocity.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption centered near S25E55 seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery starting around 2026-05-01T14:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-01T16:00Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely far-sided and related to an opening of field lines observed over the NE limb in SDO AIA 193 imagery starting around 2026-05-01T15:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to source likely beyond the limb and lack of STEREO A imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-05-01T19:08Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C2 in later frames. This event is not clearly visible in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is a region of prolonged dimming located at approx. S20E30 which was the result of an earlier eruption which began at approx. 2026-05-01T14:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-05-02T09:23Z. It might be faintly seen in SOHO LASCO C2, but it is too faint and sporadic to confirm that the feature in SOHO LASCO C2 is the same as the one in STEREO A COR2. There is not a good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky  measurement based on running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-05-02T11:24Z. It is also seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and faintly for a couple frames in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely a filament eruption centered around N16W75 to the W of AR 14424. The eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 304 and STEREO A SUVI 304 beginning around 2026-05-02T10:48Z. The eruption is also associated with a C4.0 flare that peaked at 2026-05-02T11:04Z and was centered around N16W75. There appears to be some minor southward deflection later in the propagation of the CME. There is likely also some westward deflection due to the proximity to a coronal hole that was centered at approximately N33W30 at the time of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on triangulated best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some degree of uncertainty in this measurement because this CME is partially obscured by a brighter CME in STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WSW in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-05-02T11:38Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is likely some field line movement seen beyond the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting as early as 2026-05-02T09:55Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on triangulated best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 staring around 2026-05-02T13:08Z. It is also seen to the SSW in GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. There is no good source candidate for this CME seen on the Earth-facing disk, indicating that this CME is most likely far-sided. This is consistent with triangulated measurement. It is possibly outflow following CME: 2026-04-02T11:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on triangulated best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Minor CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source has been found for this CME. It may be an outflow following CME: 2026-05-02T11:24Z.
Minor CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source has been found for this CME. It may be an outflow following CME: 2026-05-02T11:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and its source has not been found in EUV imagery, so only plane-of-sky analysis is possible for this event.

cmes.note: Very faint CME seen to the NW in early SOHO LASCO C2 imagery and disappearing after several timestamps because of faintness. Not seen in any other coronagraph. Its source is possibly the C8.7 flare most likely from AR 4420 (N16W93) behind the NW limb peaking at 2026-05-02T18:38Z and the associated very narrow eruption/ejecta seen in SDO AIA 304 and 131 and in GOES SUVI 304 to the NW behind the limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Somewhat speculative one-coronagraph analysis with early SOHO LASCO C2 imagery based on the suspected source of the flare and CME, Active Region 14420 (N16W93) that has rotated behind the NW limb.
